Adenophyllum macrophyllum is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a flowering shrub or shrub-like perennial. The plant has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Adenophyllum macrophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas and infusions, often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as to reduce fever and relieve pain.
